title: erigeron caucasicus subsp. venustus
intro: Erigeron caucasicus subsp. venustus, also known as Caucasian daisy, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its white or pink flowers and gray-green foliage. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Erigeron caucasicus subsp. venustus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable natural remedy for skin and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Erigeron caucasicus subsp. venustus can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures for topical and internal use, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
